events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Task: select correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice is wrong. Observed field condition: mechanical seal face contamination from bare-hand touch. Work staged. Reference SKF/Timken mounting guides. Need decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? It says if dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. Mechanical seal face contamination from bare hands is non-compliant; can cause seal failure, leakage. Need choose correct approach: handle mechanical seal faces only with clean lint-free gloves, use cleanroom/wrapping, clean with compatible solvent and lint-free wipes, inspect before assembly; replace if contaminated? Since staged and not begun, if seal face already contaminated by bare-hand touch, should reject/clean or replace per manufacturer. Correct material: lint-free gloves, clean lint-free wipes, approved solvent; method: avoid bare skin contact, use clean handling tools, inspect lapped faces for fingerprints/oil, clean/replace as needed.

Need fill JSON fields. decision: fail. risk: high? Mechanical seal contamination can cause premature failure, leakage, equipment damage, safety/environmental. Maybe high. work_stage: staged. component_conditions: installed-defective? The scenario says component condition categories may apply: installed-defective. But seal not installed? Work staged, materials ready. Condition is contamination from bare-hand touch, so component condition could be "non-compliant" and maybe "degraded". Use list ["non-compliant", "degraded"] maybe. They gave example includes installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Since observed condition is mechanical seal face contamination from bare-hand touch, it's non-compliant handling, possibly degraded (face contaminated). Not installed-defective because not installed. percent_complete: 0 (staged). value: 0. sound_source: ""? Not audio. confidence: 1.0. event_time: 0. rate: 0. order: [] (not asked). workable: [] maybe. findings: ["Mechanical seal faces were touched with bare hands, transferring skin oils and contaminants to lapped sealing surfaces", "Bare-hand handling violates clean-handling requirements and can cause seal leakage or premature failure"]. actions: ["Reject or quarantine contaminated seal components", "Clean seal faces per manufacturer using approved solvent and lint-free wipes only if allowed, otherwise replace", "Handle all seal faces with clean lint-free gloves and avoid direct skin contact", "Inspect lapped faces for contamination, scoring, and damage before assembly", "Re-train personnel on SKF/Timken clean mounting/handling practices"]. rationale: "Mechanical seal faces are precision-lapped and must remain free of oil, dirt, and skin contaminants; bare-hand contact introduces residues that prevent proper sealing and can cause leakage/early failure. Correct practice is to wear clean lint-free gloves, use protective wrapping, and clean/inspect faces before installation; contaminated seals should be cleaned or replaced per manufacturer guidance." references: ["SKF/Timken mounting guides - clean handling of bearings and seals", "Manufacturer mechanical seal installation instructions", "ISO 9001/quality gate standard work for contamination control"]. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "staged",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "degraded"],
  "percent_complete": 0,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Mechanical seal faces were touched with bare hands, transferring skin oils and contaminants onto precision-lapped sealing surfaces",
    "Bare-hand handling violates clean-handling standard work and creates a high risk of seal leakage or premature failure"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Quarantine the contaminated seal(s) and do not install until properly addressed",
    "Clean seal faces only with manufacturer-approved solvent and lint-free wipes if permitted; otherwise replace the seal",
    "Handle all seal faces with clean lint-free gloves and keep protective wrapping in place until installation",
    "Inspect lapped faces for contamination, scoring, or damage before assembly",
    "Re-train personnel on SKF/Timken clean mounting and contamination-control practices"
  ],
  "rationale": "Mechanical seal faces are precision-lapped and must remain free of oil, dirt, and skin contaminants. Bare-hand contact introduces residues that prevent proper sealing and can cause leakage or early failure. The correct approach is to wear clean lint-free gloves, keep faces protected until installation, and clean or replace any contaminated seal per manufacturer guidance.",
  "references": [
    "SKF/Timken mounting guides - clean handling of bearings and seals",
    "Manufacturer mechanical seal installation instructions",
    "Quality gate standard work for contamination control"
  ]
}
